meaning: n.(尤指连续的)铿锵声,丁当声
examples: He has a large bell in his hand and this he swings mechanically, awakening a deafening clangor.
Just last week, there was much clangor over the Dow (price level) surpassing its 2007 peak.
All of this is a relief from the clangor of an American thriller, but our interest in the movie ebbs when Corbijn devotes many minutes to the fashioning of a perfect rifle.
